channel.title: briefUpdatesHealthOffshootNL
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesHealthOffshootNL, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: The People — And Research — Lost in the NIH Exodus
item.description: Government data shows the National Institutes of Health lost about 4,400 people — more than 20% of its staff — as the Trump administration slashed the...

item.title: Six Federal Scientists Run Out by Trump Talk About the Work Left Undone
item.description: Cancer treatments, disease outbreaks, addiction science: Scientists say an exodus from the National Institutes of Health will harm the nation's abilit...

item.title: Taking the heat. Sauna's popular and there may be health benefits too
item.description: It's an age old practice that's having a moment right now. But is there anything to the health claims? Scientists say sauna is more than hot air.

item.title: This Doctor-Senator Who Backed RFK Jr. Now Faces a Fight for His Job — And His Legacy
item.description: A year after Sen. Bill Cassidy, a Louisiana Republican, warily cast the vote ensuring Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s ascension to Health and Human Services s...

item.title: Medical schools agree to expand their nutrition curriculum this fall
item.description: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. announced that 53 medical schools in 31 states will expand their nutrition curriculum to 40 hours. Most medical...

item.title: In lawsuit, Minnesota accuses Trump administration of 'weaponizing' Medicaid funding
item.description: The federal government said the state should do more to fight fraud and is holding back funds. Minnesota officials say the attack is unfair as the sta...

item.title: When Artificial Intelligence Starts Rewriting Reality
item.description: By BRIAN JOONDEPH Artificial intelligence is quickly becoming a core part of healthcare operations. It drafts clinical notes, summarizes patient visit...

item.title: Pregnant women in ERs took less Tylenol after Trump autism warning
item.description: A study in The Lancet finds that pregnant women in emergency rooms used less Tylenol after President Trump said it could raise their babies' risk of a...

item.title: Can a digital tablet cut back a country's overuse of antibiotics?
item.description: Overprescribing antibiotics breeds antibiotic resistance. A new tool aims to lower a notably high rate of such prescriptions in Rwanda.

item.title: What the Health? From KFF Health News: 40 Years of Health Policy
item.description: This month is 40 years since host Julie Rovner, chief Washington correspondent for KFF Health News, began reporting on health policy in Washington. To...

item.title: As ICE Moved In, Minnesotans Set Up a Shadow Medical System. It’s a Lesson for Other Cities.
item.description: President Donald Trump’s immigration crackdown in Minneapolis forced families into hiding and catalyzed informal medical networks to deliver critical ...

item.title: Oren Nissim, Brook-ai – Figuring out RPM
item.description: Oren Nissim is the CEO of Brook-ai which is making some waves in the remote care space. Their goals is to use remote patient monitoring to help provid...

item.title: Miriam Paramore, RxUtility
item.description: In this quickbite interview recorded at the Feb 2026 VIVE conference, I am talking with Miriam Paramore. Miriam is building RxUtility, which is helpin...

item.title: The Vocabulary of Survival
item.description: By GEORGE BEAUREGARD From 2018 to 2022, I served as a physician executive in a large health system on Long Island. During that period, I became acquai...

item.title: Will AI Solve Immunology’s Debate Over “Self vs. Non-Self?”
item.description: By MIKE MAGEE In 1872, English mathematician and sometimes poet, Augustus de Morgan, wrote this catching rhyme: “Great fleas have little fleas upon th...
